 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.8","title":"Implement host health checking before builds","description":"# Host Health Checking Before Builds

## Purpose
Fail fast if a host is offline or missing required capabilities. Avoid long SSH hangs and late build failures.

## Checks
- SSH connectivity (short timeout + BatchMode)
- Disk space threshold
- Toolchains (rust/go/bun)
- Docker/Colima for act (if Linux builds run on host)
- Host clock drift (optional, warn only)

## Output
- JSON summary for automation
- Cache results for short TTL (5m)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SSH check completes quickly
- [ ] Toolchains verified per host capabilities
- [ ] Clear warnings for low disk or missing deps
- [ ] Build uses only healthy hosts unless overridden","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:11.872176622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:55.107460331Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:55.107442968Z","close_reason":"Integrated host_health.sh module into dsr CLI. Added health command with check/all/clear-cache subcommands. Checks SSH connectivity, disk space, toolchains, Docker status, and clock drift. Caches results for 5 minutes. Requires yq for host config lookups.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:11.872176622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.9","title":"Implement retry and recovery logic for failed builds","description":"# Retry and Recovery Logic

## Purpose
Handle transient failures gracefully without losing state or corrupting artifacts.

## Strategy
- Exponential backoff + jitter (avoid thundering herd)
- Only retry idempotent steps (ssh, download, upload)
- Persist state to resume after process crash

## Integration
- Uses build lock/state machine (bd-1jt.2.11)
- Skips completed hosts on resume
- Partial failures do not block successful hosts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Transient failures auto-recover
- [ ] State tracked persistently
- [ ] --resume works correctly
- [ ] Clear error reporting with logs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:53.592266937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:57:38.910863048Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:53.592266937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.971484244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:16.822551477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3","title":"Theme 3: Release Upload Automation","description":"# Release Upload Automation

## Purpose
After local builds complete, create GitHub releases and upload binaries with proper checksums and signatures.

## The dsr release Command
```bash
dsr release <tool> <version>           # Create release, upload all builds
dsr release <tool> <version> --draft   # Create as draft first
dsr release <tool> <version> --notes "..." # Custom release notes
```

## Release Workflow

### 1. Verify Builds Exist
```bash
# Check all expected artifacts exist
build_dir="$DSR_STATE_DIR/builds/$tool/$version"
for target in linux-amd64 linux-arm64 darwin-arm64 darwin-amd64 windows-amd64; do
  artifact="$build_dir/${tool}-${version}-${target}"
  [ -f "$artifact" ] || die "Missing: $artifact"
done
```

### 2. Generate Checksums (No global cd)
```bash
# Generate checksums in place
sha256sum "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-* > "$build_dir/${tool}-${version}-checksums.sha256"
```

### 3. Sign Artifacts (Optional but Recommended)
```bash
# Using minisign
for f in "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-*; do
  minisign -Sm "$f" -s ~/.minisign/key.sec
done
```

### 4. Create GitHub Release
```bash
gh release create "$version" \
  --repo "Dicklesworthstone/$tool" \
  --title "$tool $version" \
  --notes "$release_notes" \
  "$build_dir"/${tool}-${version}-*
```

### 5. Verify Upload
```bash
gh release view "$version" --repo "Dicklesworthstone/$tool" --json assets
```

## Release Notes Generation (Git Plumbing)
```bash
repo_dir="/data/projects/$tool"
last_tag=$(git -C "$repo_dir" describe --tags --abbrev=0 HEAD^)
git -C "$repo_dir" log --oneline ${last_tag}..HEAD > "$build_dir/release_notes.txt"
```

## Asset Naming Convention
```
{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}[.exe]
{tool}-{version}-checksums.sha256
{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}.minisig
```

Examples:
```
ntm-v1.5.1-linux-amd64
ntm-v1.5.1-darwin-arm64
ntm-v1.5.1-windows-amd64.exe
ntm-v1.5.1-checksums.sha256
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Creates release with correct tag
- [ ] All binaries uploaded and downloadable
- [ ] Checksums file included
- [ ] Release notes auto-generated if not provided
- [ ] Works for all 16 tools

## Error Handling
- Release already exists → Prompt to update or abort
- Upload fails → Retry, then report which assets failed
- Tag doesn't exist → Create tag or error","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:26.299668699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:49:45.249464757Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:09:26.299668699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.3.1","title":"Implement dsr release command - upload artifacts to GitHub","description":"# Implement `dsr release` — Upload Artifacts to GitHub

## Purpose
Create a GitHub Release from locally built artifacts, with retries, verification, and consistent metadata.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr release <tool> <version>
 dsr release <tool> <version> --artifacts <dir>
 dsr release <tool> <version> --draft
 dsr release <tool> <version> --generate-notes
 dsr release <tool> <version> --verify-tag
 dsr release <tool> <version> --dry-run
 dsr release <tool> <version> --resume
```

## Required Inputs
- Build manifest (source of truth)
- Checksums + signatures
- Optional SBOM + provenance

## Release Flow
1. Validate manifest + checksums
2. Create release tag (verify exists)
3. Upload binaries + checksums + signatures
4. Upload SBOM + provenance if configured
5. Verify assets match manifest

## Idempotency / Resume
- Safe to re-run (`gh release upload --clobber`)
- Track partial uploads in state for resume

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Release created with correct tag & notes
- [ ] All assets uploaded and verified
- [ ] Works for all tools
- [ ] JSON report includes asset list + validation results
- [ ] Resume re-uploads only missing/failed assets","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:53.575381627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:16:57.419506146Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-11r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:09:50.900049447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:02.228966845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:41.414912067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:22.386430911Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:53.575381627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:52.882491227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:26:54.944683486Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:53.751889231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:52:05.559597020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.10","title":"Implement release verification + retry/resume uploads","description":"# Implement Release Verification + Retry/Resume Uploads

## Purpose
Ensure GitHub releases contain the exact expected assets, and recover safely from partial upload failures.

## Verification Steps
- Compare release assets against build manifest
- Verify checksums.sha256 matches uploaded binaries
- Optionally re-download and verify sha256 for spot checks

## Retry Strategy
- Use `gh release upload --clobber` for failed assets
- Track failed uploads in state file for resume
- Idempotent uploads (safe to re-run)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Missing assets detected and reported
- [ ] Partial uploads can be resumed safely
- [ ] Release verification produces JSON report
- [ ] Re-running verification does not create duplicate assets","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:58.956655125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:55:33.160035551Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:12.415821448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:22.651659081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:58.956655125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:40.580783022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.11","title":"Add pre-release quality gates (typecheck/lint/test) per tool","description":"# Add Pre-Release Quality Gates (typecheck/lint/test) per Tool

## Purpose
Ensure every release passes the tool's own quality checks before upload to GitHub.

## Behavior
- Read `checks` from `repos.d/<tool>.yaml`
- Run checks in deterministic order
- Support `--skip-checks` and `--dry-run`
- Log per-check duration + exit code
- Fail release if any check fails (exit 1/6 depending on stage)

## Output
- stderr: human-readable summary
- stdout (JSON): list of checks, durations, exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checks run per-tool before release upload
- [ ] Failures block release with clear errors
- [ ] JSON output includes per-check results
- [ ] Dry-run shows planned checks","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:06.086111454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:27:28.402142395Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:27:28.402125253Z","close_reason":"Implemented src/quality_gates.sh module with qg_run_checks() and qg_get_checks(). Added 'dsr quality' command with --dry-run, --skip-checks, --work-dir options. Reads checks from repos.yaml, logs per-check results with durations, returns JSON with passed/failed counts. Exit 1 on failure.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:21.256627491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:47.402939311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:06.086111454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.2","title":"Update Homebrew and Scoop formulas after release","description":"# Update Homebrew and Scoop Formulas After Release

## Purpose
Update package manager metadata so users can install via `brew` and `scoop` with correct URLs + SHA256.

## Homebrew
- Update formula in `homebrew-tap`
- Use temp workdir in `/tmp` (never `/data/projects`)
- Validate with `brew audit --strict` and `brew style` when available
- Respect `--dry-run` (plan only)

## Scoop
- Update manifest JSON in `scoop-bucket`
- Use `sha256:<hex>` format
- Use temp workdir in `/tmp`
- Validate JSON and run `scoop checkver` when available

## Logging / Output
- stderr: human-readable summary
- stdout: JSON (when `--json`) listing updated formula/manifest paths + checksums

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Formula/manifest updated with correct version + hashes
- [ ] Dry-run available and deterministic
- [ ] Validation commands executed when available
- [ ] Works for all tools without touching `/data/projects`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:34.632986314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:56.629580772Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:34.632986314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:26:56.108363071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:56.629546588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"bd-36r","title":"Unit tests for quality_gates.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for quality_gates.sh Module

## Module Overview
quality_gates.sh provides pre-release quality checks:
- `quality_run_checks` - Execute all configured checks
- `quality_parse_config` - Read checks from repos.d/*.yaml
- `quality_format_results` - Output results in JSON/human formats
- `quality_should_skip` - Handle --skip-checks flag

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Check Execution Tests
- Run single check command (e.g., `cargo test`)
- Run multiple checks in sequence
- Capture stdout/stderr per check
- Measure duration per check
- Handle check timeout

### 2. Config Parsing Tests
- Parse checks array from YAML
- Handle missing checks section (no-op)
- Validate check command format
- Support check-specific options

### 3. Result Formatting Tests
- JSON output matches schema
- Human output includes pass/fail indicators
- Duration shown per check
- Exit code summary at end

### 4. Skip Logic Tests
- --skip-checks skips all checks
- --skip-checks=lint skips specific check
- --dry-run shows planned checks
- Non-interactive mode continues on failure

### 5. Error Handling Tests
- Check command not found → clear error
- Check returns non-zero → record failure
- Timeout reached → record timeout
- Continue after failure (report at end)

## Test Approach
- Create temp repos.d with test configs
- Use simple check commands (true, false, sleep)
- Verify JSON schema compliance
- Test all exit code scenarios","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:56.002943319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:48:13.544549533Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-36r","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:56.002943319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-36r","title":"Unit tests for quality_gates.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for quality_gates.sh Module

## Module Overview
quality_gates.sh provides pre-release quality checks:
- `quality_run_checks` - Execute all configured checks
- `quality_parse_config` - Read checks from repos.d/*.yaml
- `quality_format_results` - Output results in JSON/human formats
- `quality_should_skip` - Handle --skip-checks flag

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Check Execution Tests
- Run single check command (e.g., `cargo test`)
- Run multiple checks in sequence
- Capture stdout/stderr per check
- Measure duration per check
- Handle check timeout

### 2. Config Parsing Tests
- Parse checks array from YAML
- Handle missing checks section (no-op)
- Validate check command format
- Support check-specific options

### 3. Result Formatting Tests
- JSON output matches schema
- Human output includes pass/fail indicators
- Duration shown per check
- Exit code summary at end

### 4. Skip Logic Tests
- --skip-checks skips all checks
- --skip-checks=lint skips specific check
- --dry-run shows planned checks
- Non-interactive mode continues on failure

### 5. Error Handling Tests
- Check command not found → clear error
- Check returns non-zero → record failure
- Timeout reached → record timeout
- Continue after failure (report at end)

## Test Approach
- Create temp repos.d with test configs

 {"id":"bd-39w","title":"E2E: dsr doctor (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr doctor (real behavior)

## Purpose
Verify doctor checks real dependencies and produces actionable output.

## Approach
- Run doctor in a clean env and capture missing deps.
- Run with `--json` to validate schema and stream separation.

## Logging
- Log detected deps and their status.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Missing deps are reported clearly.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.
- [ ] Non-interactive mode does not prompt.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:56:15.972960698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:52.165070220Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39w","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:50.126884944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-39w","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:52.163395945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3cn","title":"Unit tests for github.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for github.sh Module

## Module Overview
github.sh provides GitHub API access with caching and rate limiting:
- `gh_check` - Verify gh CLI + authentication
- `gh_check_token` - Validate GITHUB_TOKEN
- `gh_api` - Make API requests with caching
- `gh_workflow_runs` - List workflow runs
- `gh_releases` - List releases
- `gh_create_release` - Create a release
- `gh_upload_asset` - Upload release asset

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Authentication Tests
- `gh_check` succeeds when gh is authenticated
- `gh_check` fails gracefully when gh not installed
- `gh_check` fails gracefully when not authenticated
- `gh_check_token` validates GITHUB_TOKEN format
- `gh_check_token` detects expired/invalid tokens (via API call)

### 2. Caching Tests
- Cache directory created under XDG_CACHE_HOME
- First request creates cache entry
- Second request within TTL uses cache
- Request after TTL expiry fetches fresh
- ETag-based validation (304 Not Modified)
- Cache key includes endpoint parameters

### 3. Rate Limiting Tests
- Rate limit detection via response headers
- Retry with backoff on 429 response
- Maximum retry count enforced (3)
- Rate limit warning logged

### 4. Error Handling Tests
- Network errors produce clear messages
- 404 responses handled correctly
- 401 (auth) errors trigger re-auth hint
- 500 errors trigger retry logic

### 5. JSON Output Tests
- `--json` mode returns valid JSON
- Error responses follow envelope schema
- Success responses include metadata

## Test Approach
- Test validation/formatting logic WITHOUT live API calls
- Use skip markers for tests requiring network
- Cache tests use temp directory
- Rate limit tests use mock time if needed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:45.881678501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:14:18.130245753Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:14:18.129628029Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cn","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:45.881678501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
